skill.name: senior-data-engineer
skill.description: Data engineering guidance for scalable pipelines, ETL and ELT workflows, data modeling, DataOps practices, and production infrastructure planning.
skill.summary: Plan, review, and improve production data engineering workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography and network reconnaissance alerts are false positives from prose and labels. The skill does include runnable Python, Docker, Kubernetes, and Helm command examples, which creates medium operational risk if users execute them without review. No evidence found for prompt injection, credential exfiltration, network callbacks, or destructive behavior.

medium_findings.title: Documented External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md includes bash examples that run local Python scripts and deployment tools including Docker, kubectl, and Helm. These are not automatically executed by the skill, but users could run commands that affect local projects or clusters.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and semantically meaningful, but they are documentation examples rather than hidden execution logic. Risk depends on user execution and local environment permissions.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain ordinary documentation text, headings, or argparse descriptions. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, cipher configuration, hashing call, or cryptographic implementation was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the cited lines are prose such as design headings, scalability notes, and CLI descriptions. The files do not import cryptography libraries or call weak algorithms at the cited lines.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations describe resource awareness in reference documentation. No port scanning, host discovery, socket use, network request, or reconnaissance command was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are identical documentation bullets about resource awareness. They contain no executable network behavior or network tooling instructions.

low_findings.title: Reference File Links Misclassified As External Commands
low_findings.description: Several static external command alerts point to markdown references to local documentation files. These links do not execute commands and are safe documentation navigation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain markdown references to files under references/. There is no shell invocation, subprocess usage, or command syntax at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Operational Commands In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents commands for Python scripts, tests, formatters, Docker builds, Kubernetes applies, Helm upgrades, and log access. These commands are visible to users, but they can change infrastructure when copied into a shell.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Commands are clearly present and include deployment tooling. They are not concealed or auto-executed, so the concern is operational misuse rather than malware.

content.value_statement: Data teams need repeatable guidance for pipeline design, quality checks, modeling, and operations. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code reason through production data engineering work.
actual_capabilities: Guides architecture decisions for batch, real-time, and distributed data pipelines.
Provides reference material for data modeling, DataOps, reliability, monitoring, and governance.
Offers scaffolded Python CLI scripts for pipeline orchestration, data quality, and ETL optimization.
Reviews performance, scalability, reliability, and cost trade-offs for data workflows.
Suggests production practices for testing, deployment, observability, and incident readiness.
limitations: The included Python scripts are placeholders and do not implement real validation or optimization logic.
The skill does not connect to databases, warehouses, object stores, or orchestration services by itself.
Deployment commands in the documentation require local review before use in any environment.
It cannot verify compliance requirements without project-specific policies and data classifications.

use_cases.title: Design a New Data Platform
use_cases.description: Create a practical architecture for ingestion, transformation, quality checks, orchestration, and monitoring.

use_cases.title: Review an Existing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Identify bottlenecks, reliability gaps, missing tests, and weak operational practices in a production workflow.

use_cases.title: Plan DataOps Standards
use_cases.description: Define repeatable conventions for testing, releases, observability, ownership, and incident response.

prompt_templates.title: Explain My Pipeline Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first production data pipeline.
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a data pipeline for [source] into [destination]. Explain the main architecture options, trade-offs, and a simple recommended path.

prompt_templates.title: Review My ETL Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user validating a proposed workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this ETL design for scalability, reliability, observability, and cost. Call out risks and suggest concrete improvements: [design details].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Data Quality Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Practitioner adding validation to a critical dataset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a data quality plan for [dataset]. Include checks, severity levels, alerting, ownership, and how failures should block or warn downstream jobs.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize a Data Platform
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user planning a migration or platform redesign.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this current data platform and propose a modernization roadmap. Cover orchestration, modeling, lineage, governance, performance, migration risk, and rollout phases: [context].

output_examples.input: Review a nightly customer ETL pipeline that often misses its service level target.
output: A prioritized diagnosis of likely bottlenecks and reliability risks.
A remediation plan covering partitioning, retries, data quality gates, alerts, and ownership.
A rollout sequence that separates low-risk observability work from deeper architecture changes.

output_examples.input: Design a data model for product analytics events across web and mobile applications.
output: A recommended event schema and naming convention.
Guidance for slowly changing dimensions, identity resolution, and late-arriving events.
Testing and governance checks for consistency across producers.

output_examples.input: Create DataOps standards for a small analytics engineering team.
output: A concise operating model for code review, testing, deployment, and incident response.
A monitoring checklist for freshness, volume, schema drift, and downstream impact.
A maturity roadmap that starts with high-value controls before adding advanced automation.

best_practices: Share concrete pipeline context, data volumes, service level targets, and failure examples before asking for recommendations.
Ask the skill to separate immediate fixes from architecture changes that need migration planning.
Review every generated command or deployment step before using it against local systems or clusters.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the placeholder scripts as complete production tools without implementing the missing logic.
Do not run documentation commands against shared infrastructure without environment-specific validation.
Do not request generic best practices when a specific pipeline, dataset, or operational problem is available.

faq.question: Does this skill build complete data pipelines automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, reviews, and scaffolding. The included scripts are placeholders that require implementation.

faq.question: Can it help with both batch and streaming systems?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation covers batch, real-time, distributed processing, reliability, and monitoring considerations.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the commands in SKILL.md?
faq.answer: Review them first. Some examples use deployment tools that can affect local projects or Kubernetes environments.

faq.question: Does it access my data warehouse or cloud account?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not make external connections by itself and contains no configured credentials.

faq.question: What information should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide sources, destinations, volumes, latency needs, failure modes, compliance constraints, and current tooling.

faq.question: Can it support governance and compliance planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure questions and controls, but final requirements need review by your compliance owner.
